title: New vehicles of Chapter 87 (Tariff notice 3)
body: "New explanatory notes  A new Combined Nomenclature Explanatory Note was published on 13 January 2025.  Explanatory Notes to the Combined Nomenclature of the European Union.  (C/2025/330)  This is not considered a change in Northern Ireland practice.  Classification (Combined Nomenclature (CN) code)  Chapter 87  Reason  Pursuant to Article 9(1), point (a), of Council Regulation (EEC) No 2658/87, the Explanatory notes to the Combined Nomenclature of the European Union are amended as follows:  On page 374, in the explanatory note to Chapter 87, ‘Vehicles other than railway or tramway rolling stock, and parts and accessories thereof’, ‘General’ points 1 and 2 are replaced by the following:   Throughout Chapter 87 of the Combined Nomenclature, “new vehicles” means:  (a) vehicles which have not been permanently registered for more than 6 months, regardless of the number of kilometres they have been driven, or (b) vehicles which have not been driven for more than 6000 kilometres, regardless of the time they have been permanently registered.   Temporary registration of the vehicles for the purpose of transport to the place where they will be permanently registered is not considered as permanent registration.  2. Throughout Chapter 87 of the Combined Nomenclature, “used vehicles” means vehicles which do not fulfil the definition of “new” vehicles under point 1.  Contact details   Tariff ClassificationCustoms Policy & Strategy3rd Floor14 Westfield AvenueStratfordLondonE20 1HZ   Email: tariff.classification@hmrc.gov.uk  This Tariff notice is published for information purposes only. "

taxons.title: UK Trade Tariff and classification of goods
